It is nothing new for the most righteous people and the most righteous cause to meet with enemies. This comes from the old hatred in the seed of the serpent against the Seed of the woman. David in his troubles, Christ in his suffering, the church under persecution, and the Christian during times of temptation all ask God Almighty to appear on their behalf and to defend their cause. We are quick to justify feeling uneasy about the injuries people do us, by thinking we never gave them reason to treat us so badly; but this should make us calm, because then we can more confidently expect that God will plead our case. David prayed to God to reveal himself in his trial. Let me have inner comfort under all outward troubles, to strengthen my soul. If God, through his Spirit, assures our spirits that he is our salvation, we need desire nothing more to make us happy. If God is our friend, it does not matter who is our enemy. Through the spirit of prophecy, David foretells the just judgments of God that will come upon his enemies because of their great wickedness. These are predictions; they look forward and show the doom of the enemies of Christ and his kingdom. We must not desire or pray for the ruin of any enemies except our own desires and the evil spirits that would try to destroy us. A traveler stranded at night on a bad road is an expressive image of a sinner walking in the slippery and dangerous ways of temptation. But David, having committed his cause to God, did not doubt his own deliverance. The bones are the strongest parts of the body. The psalmist here resolves to serve and glorify God with all his strength. If such language can be applied to outward salvation, how much more will it apply to heavenly things in Christ Jesus!

Call a man ungrateful, and you cannot call him anything worse: this was the character of David's enemies. In this way he was like Christ. David shows how kindly he had behaved towards them during times of trouble. We ought to mourn for the sins of those who do not mourn for themselves. We will not lose by the good things we do for anyone, no matter how ungrateful they may be. Let us learn to keep our souls in patience and gentleness like David, or rather after Christ’s example.

Although the people of God are, and try to be, peaceful, it has been common for their enemies to plan deceptive things against them. David prays, My soul is in danger, Lord, rescue it; it belongs to you, the Father of spirits, so claim your own; it is yours, save it! Lord, do not be far from me as if I were a stranger. He who exalted the once suffering Redeemer will appear for all his people: the roaring lion will not destroy their souls any more than he could that of Christ, their Surety. They trust their souls in his hands; they are one with him through faith, are precious in his sight, and will be rescued from destruction so that they may give thanks in heaven.
